subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **45 double-ended cards** — Each card has two different numbers (one on each end). Cards range from 1-10.
- **5 Scout & Show tokens** (1 per player)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle all 45 cards.**
2. **Deal evenly** based on player count (some cards may be removed).
3. **IMPORTANT** — Pick up your hand without rearranging the card order! You may flip the entire hand upside down once, but cannot reorder individual cards.
4. **Each player** takes 1 Scout & Show token.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Hold cards as dealt** — Do NOT rearrange card order.
- **Choose orientation** — You may flip your entire hand once to pick which end of each card faces up.
- **1 Scout & Show token** — Can be used once per round.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Scout is a climbing card game where you can't rearrange your hand. Play sets or sequences to beat the current play, or scout (take) a card from the current play to improve your hand. First to empty their hand or the last player standing scores.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Show** — Play a set of cards that beats the current showing.
2. **Scout** — Take 1 card from the current showing and add it to your hand.
3. **Scout & Show** — Do both (once per round, using your token).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Showing Cards
- Play a group of adjacent cards from your hand.
- Must beat the current showing by being either:
  - **Same number of cards, higher value** (sets beat same-size sets of lower value; runs beat same-size runs of lower value)
  - **More cards** (any valid group of more cards beats fewer cards)
- Valid groups: single card, set of same number, or consecutive run.
- Cards played must be adjacent in your hand.

#### Scouting
- Take 1 card from either end of the current showing.
- Choose which orientation (which number faces up).
- Insert it anywhere in your hand.
- The player who showed those cards gets 1 point chip.

#### Scout & Show Token
- Once per round, Scout first then immediately Show.
- Flip your token to used side.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **No rearranging** — You never reorder cards in your hand (except inserting scouted cards).
- **Double-ended cards** — When scouting, choose which end faces up.
- **Adjacency** — Cards played must be next to each other in your hand.
- **Round end** — If all other players consecutively scout (no one beats you), the round ends.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Round End
- A player empties their hand, OR
- All other players scout consecutively (no one beats the current show).

#### Scoring
- **+1 point** per card you showed that opponents scouted from.
- **-1 point** per card remaining in your hand.
- The player who went out (or whose show wasn't beaten) gains bonus points.

#### Game End
- Play rounds equal to the number of players. Highest total score wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Initial flip matters** — Look at both orientations of your hand carefully before choosing.
- **Scout to improve** — Insert scouted cards strategically to create adjacency for big plays.
- **Save your token** — Use Scout & Show at a pivotal moment.
- **Big plays early** — Playing many cards at once is hard to beat.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Build runs** — Scout cards to extend consecutive sequences in your hand.
- **Timing** — Let others show first, then beat them with a stronger play.
- **Scout from weakness** — When scouting, take cards that deny opponents while helping you.
- **Go out fast** — Points from remaining cards are penalties; prioritize emptying your hand.
